votes.reason: The Python code correctly implements the Coq specification by checking if r is -1 when x > y or when there are no even numbers in the range [x, y], and otherwise checking if r is the maximum even number in the range [x, y].
The Python code correctly computes the maximum even number in the range [x, y] and checks if r equals it, or -1 if no such number exists or x > y, matching the Coq specification.

The Python code correctly identifies the maximum even number in the range [x, y] and checks if r equals it, or returns r == -1 when no such number exists or x > y, exactly matching the Coq specification.

The Coq spec says: if x > y, return -1; if x <= y and there exists an even number in [x,y], return the largest such even; otherwise return -1. The Python code computes max_even as the largest even <= y, then checks if max_even >= x (i.e., if there's an even in [x,y]). If so, it returns that max_even; otherwise -1. This matches the Coq spec's three cases exactly for all concrete inputs.

The Coq spec says: if x > y, return -1; if x <= y and there exists an even number in [x,y], return the largest such even; otherwise return -1. The Python code computes max_even as the largest even <= y, then checks if it's >= x (i.e., in range). If so, r must equal max_even (the largest even in range); otherwise r = -1. This matches the Coq spec's concrete behavior for all inputs.

The Coq spec says: if x > y, return -1; if x <= y and there exists an even number in [x,y], return the maximum such even (which is y if y is even, else y-1, provided it's >= x); otherwise return -1. The Python code computes exactly this: when x > y it checks r == -1; when x <= y it computes max_even as the largest even <= y, and if max_even >= x (meaning an even exists in range), checks r == max_even, otherwise checks r == -1. This matches the Coq specification on all concrete inputs.

Both require r = -1 when x > y or when no even exists in [x, y], and otherwise r must be the maximum even in [x, y]. The Python code computes that maximum as y (if even) or y-1 and checks it lies within [x, y], matching the Coq spec for all integers, including negatives.

Both specify r = -1 if x > y or if no even exists in [x, y]; otherwise r must be the maximum even in [x, y]. The Python code computes y if even else y-1, checks it is >= x, and requires r to equal that; this matches the Coq spec on all integers.
Matches Coq: returns -1 if x>y or no even in [x,y]; otherwise returns the largest even in [x,y] (y if even, else y-1).
